title: metrosideros polymorpha var. pseudorugosa
intro: Metrosideros polymorpha var. pseudorugosa, also known as the Ma'o hau hele, is a variety of the New Zealand native tree Metrosideros polymorpha. It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and protection against infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Ma'o hau hele are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
